Command: /bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import chess, functools, time
fen='3r2k1/pp3pp1/4b2p/7Q/3n4/PqBBR2P/5PP1/6K1 b - - 0 1'
root=chess.Board(fen)

def key(board):
    return board.transposition_key() if hasattr(board,'transposition_key') else (board.board_fen(), board.turn, board.castling_rights, board.ep_square)

nodes=0
pv_cache={}

def move_score(board, m):
    score=0
    if board.gives_check(m): score += 10000
    if board.is_capture(m): score += 1000
    if board.piece_at(m.to_square) and board.piece_at(m.to_square).piece_type==chess.KING: score += 99999
    # prefer queen moves maybe
    p=board.piece_at(m.from_square)
    if p: score += p.piece_type
    return score

from functools import lru_cache

def search(depth):
    @lru_cache(None)
    def can(fen_key, depth):
        global nodes
        board=chess.Board(fen_key)
        nodes += 1
        if board.is_checkmate():
            return board.turn == chess.WHITE # white mated by black
        if board.is_stalemate() or board.is_insufficient_material() or depth<=0:
            return False
        moves=list(board.legal_moves)
        moves.sort(key=lambda m: move_score(board,m), reverse=(board.turn==chess.BLACK))
        if board.turn == chess.BLACK:
            for m in moves:
                bb=board.copy(stack=False); bb.push(m)
                if can(bb.fen(), depth-1):
                    pv_cache[(fen_key, depth)] = m.uci()
                    return True
            return False
        else:
            # White may avoid mate; black force only if all white moves still lose
            for m in moves:
                bb=board.copy(stack=False); bb.push(m)
                if not can(bb.fen(), depth-1):
                    return False
            return True
    winning=[]
    for m in sorted(root.legal_moves, key=lambda m: move_score(root,m), reverse=True):
        bb=root.copy(stack=False); bb.push(m)
        if can(bb.fen(), depth-1):
            winning.append(root.san(m))
    print('depth', depth, 'nodes', nodes, 'winning first moves', winning[:20], 'count', len(winning))
    return winning, can
for d in range(1,12):
    nodes=0
    t=time.time()
    w,can=search(d)
    print('time', time.time()-t)
    if w:
        break
PY"
